'''Codoran''' was a precious metal used to construct jewelry,<ref name="CSWE" /> such as a [[codoran ring]].<ref name="SPP">''[[Specter of the Past]]''</ref> In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],<ref name="TERC">The events of ''[[Specter of the Past]]'' take place in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''.</ref> [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Bemos]], a member of the [[Imperial Remnant]]'s [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]], wore a large codoran [[Ring/Legends|ring]] on his finger while attending a meeting with the Moff council and Imperial [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] on the<ref name="SPP" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]].<ref name="SPP" />

==Behind the scenes==
Codoran first appeared in the [[1997]] novel ''[[Specter of the Past]]'', the first volume of [[Timothy Zahn]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Hand of Thrawn Duology]]''.<ref name="SPP" /> Codoran later appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:C" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> The game misspelled the substance as "codoan."<ref name="SWG:C" />

[[File:MineralResource.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A crate of codoran coppper.]]
In the game, codoran was a type of [[W:c:swg:Resource|resource]] that could be used by [[W:c:swg:Crafting|crafters]] to create items. Codoran was one of the many types of [[W:c:swg:Copper|copper]] that could be obtained by [[W:c:swg:Harvester|harvesting]] the soil of the game's worlds. The type of copper collected during harvesting was determined randomly, and all varieties of coppers were interchangeable and had similar characteristics. Like other resources in the game, codoran was only represented visually as a generic crate of resources.<ref name="SWG:C" />
